title: Interactive Calculator REPL
description: Build an interactive calculator that supports arithmetic expressions with operator precedence, parentheses, and named variables. Implements a tokenizer, parser, and evaluator.
requirements: Tokenize input into numbers, operators, parentheses, and identifiers
Parse expressions respecting operator precedence (* and / before + and -)
Provide an interactive REPL loop that prints results
Handle errors: division by zero, unknown variables, syntax errors
hints: Tokenize first: convert characters into a stream of Token enums
Use recursive descent parsing: expression -> term -> factor -> primary
Terms handle * and /, expressions handle + and - (this gives correct precedence)
Substitute variable names with their values before parsing the expression
For the REPL, use std::io::stdin().read_line() in a loop
bonusChallenge: Add support for functions like sqrt(), sin(), cos(). Add the ability to define custom functions. Implement expression history with up-arrow recall.
